title: [lichen_l2.c:497-512] lichen_l2_recv returns NET_CONTINUE unconditionally
description:
Gap found: L2 receive callback semantics not reviewed.

Why this was missed: Considered boilerplate, not analyzed.

The actual bug: The lichen_l2_recv() callback (lines 498-513) always returns NET_CONTINUE. The comment (lines 486-496) explains why: 'Packet is already an IPv6 packet (decompressed in lichen_l2_input). Let the IP layer handle it.'

This is correct for LICHEN's design. However, the callback could return:
- NET_DROP: to reject the packet at L2 (e.g., invalid L2 checksum)
- NET_OK: to indicate L2 consumed the packet (not applicable here)
- NET_CONTINUE: pass to IP layer

Since all L2 validation happens in lichen_l2_input() before the packet reaches net_recv_data(), returning NET_CONTINUE is correct. The L2 recv callback is only called for packets that already passed L2 validation.

Verified correct. Priority 4 - documentation note.
close_reason: Fixed: Added 'WHY NET_CONTINUE IS ALWAYS RETURNED' section explaining L2 validation happens in lichen_l2_input before this callback
